description:
You are acting as a release-manager automation assistant.  
Inside the instance you will find two already-existing directory trees that hold build artefacts and deployment history:

1. /home/user/releases  
   • /home/user/releases/release_2023-09-01/build.info  
   • /home/user/releases/release_2023-09-08/build.info  

2. /home/user/deployments  
   • /home/user/deployments/deployment_status.log  

Your job is to derive two **new** text files (one CSV, one log) by parsing those sources with awk and/or sed.  
The whole task can be accomplished with a simple series of two to three shell commands; do not create any additional helper scripts—plain one-liners are enough.

A. CSV summary of build versions  
------------------------------------------------
File to create: /home/user/releases/release_versions.csv

Required format (exact – automated tests will diff byte-for-byte):

• First row must be a header literally reading:  
  Release,Version

• One subsequent row per release directory that exists directly under /home/user/releases.  
  The release folder name goes into the first column (“Release”).  
  Extract the value after “VERSION:” on the second line of each build.info file and place it in the second column (“Version”).  
  Rows must be sorted in **lexicographical order of the release folder name**.  

Example (the real values for this task are shown in the table below only for illustration – your output must of course match them exactly):

Release,Version  
release_2023-01-15,v1.2.0  
release_2023-02-13,v1.3.1  

B. Log of failed deployments  
------------------------------------------------
File to create: /home/user/deployments/failed_deployments.log

Required format (exact – automated tests will diff byte-for-byte):

• Include one line for every entry in deployment_status.log whose STATUS field is literally “FAILED”.  
• Strip the square brackets and colon so the line becomes three comma-separated fields:  
  TIMESTAMP,RELEASE,STATUS

  For example the source line  
    [2023-09-08 13:15] release_2023-09-08: FAILED  
  must be transformed to  
    2023-09-08 13:15,release_2023-09-08,FAILED

• Preserve the original order in which the FAILED entries appear in deployment_status.log.  
• Do not prepend a header line: the log file consists only of the transformed data lines.

------------------------------------------------
Quick checklist (all will be automatically verified):

1. /home/user/releases/release_versions.csv exists.  
2. First line of that CSV is exactly “Release,Version”.  
3. Each subsequent line contains “release_YYYY-MM-DD” followed by a comma, followed by the version string taken from the matching build.info.  
4. Lines are sorted alphabetically (simple string sort) by the release folder name.  
5. /home/user/deployments/failed_deployments.log exists and contains exactly one line per FAILED record, formatted as “TIMESTAMP,RELEASE,FAILED”.  
6. No extra whitespace, blank lines or trailing spaces anywhere in the two new files.  

Hints (do NOT echo these literally in your commands – they are just conceptual guidance):
• Use awk -F': ' '/^VERSION:/ { print $2 }' build.info to capture version numbers.  
• Use sed -n 's/^\[\(.*\)\] \(.*\): \(FAILED\)$/\1,\2,\3/p' deployment_status.log to transform failed lines.  

Once both files exist with the specified contents, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial state provided to the agent
-----------------------------------

Directory tree:
/home/user/releases/
└── release_2023-09-01
    └── build.info
└── release_2023-09-08
    └── build.info

Contents of /home/user/releases/release_2023-09-01/build.info  (final newline at end of file):
BUILD_ID: 202309011230
VERSION: v1.4.2
COMMIT: 1a2b3c4d

Contents of /home/user/releases/release_2023-09-08/build.info  (final newline at end of file):
BUILD_ID: 202309081305
VERSION: v1.5.0
COMMIT: 5d6e7f8g

Directory /home/user/deployments contains deployment_status.log with the exact contents (final newline at end of file):
[2023-09-01 12:30] release_2023-09-01: SUCCESS
[2023-09-01 12:45] release_2023-09-01: DEPLOYED
[2023-09-08 13:15] release_2023-09-08: FAILED
[2023-09-08 13:40] release_2023-09-08: ROLLBACK_COMPLETED

Expected artefacts after the agent completes
--------------------------------------------

1. /home/user/releases/release_versions.csv  (must end with a single trailing newline):
Release,Version
release_2023-09-01,v1.4.2
release_2023-09-08,v1.5.0

2. /home/user/deployments/failed_deployments.log  (must end with a single trailing newline):
2023-09-08 13:15,release_2023-09-08,FAILED

No other files, modifications or output artefacts are required.
